response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software developer with strong web, mobile, and backend experience, but the role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear on the resume. There is no evidence of GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, performance analysis at the hardware level, or chip-design collaboration. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments is the closest adjacent experience but is far from Principal-level hardware architecture work.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background across many domains
Some low-level C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship, TDD, and testing discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning optimization background
Lacks the advanced degree or equivalent hardware-architecture depth typical for a Principal Performance Architect

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN optimization experience
